# gaming - SEO + GEO Remediation Playbook

**Target**: https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai
**Backing service**: aicart/intelliverse-gaming-backend (image `intelliverse-gaming-backend:latest`)
**Source repo (best guess)**: intelli-verse-x/intelliverse-gaming-backend
**Audit reference**: `reports/gaming.md`
**Generated**: 2026-05-17T18:25:00Z
**Generated by**: gastown audits/crew/auditor (firecrawl + claude)

---

## 1. Executive summary

The gaming subdomain returns HTTP 404 with an empty body—DNS resolves, TLS connects, but no application is deployed or backend is misconfigured. This is identical to the products.intelli-verse-x.ai issue: search engines mark it as dead link, AI systems have no information. Fix depends on intent: (a) deploy gaming platform with SEO metadata, (b) serve "Coming Soon" with noindex, or (c) redirect/remove. Projected impact: 0/10 → 7/10 LLM-friendliness once live gaming platform is deployed with Game/VideoGame schemas.

## 2. Current state recap

From `reports/gaming.md`:

- ✗ **HTTP accessibility**: Returns HTTP 404 with empty body (CRITICAL)
- ✗ **All SEO/GEO checks**: N/A — no content served

Status suggests: under construction, misconfigured deployment, or abandoned subdomain.

## 3. 2026 best-practice baseline

Same as products.md remediation—see `remediation/products.md` sections 3 for full research citations.

**Gaming-specific additions**:

**Source**: [Schema.org - VideoGame](https://schema.org/VideoGame) (2026)

For gaming platforms, use `VideoGame` or `Game` schemas:
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "VideoGame",
  "name": "Game Title",
  "description": "...",
  "genre": "Educational / Puzzle / Adventure",
  "gamePlatform": "Web Browser",
  "offers": {
    "@type": "Offer",
    "price": "0",
    "priceCurrency": "USD"
  }
}
```

## 4. Concrete remediation (prioritized)

### Scenario A: Deploy active gaming platform

### 1. Deploy the gaming application

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 4-8 hours
- **What**: Deploy intelliverse-gaming-backend to web server
- **Why**: Fixes HTTP 404, enables indexing
- **Where**: `intelli-verse-x/intelliverse-gaming-backend` repo
- **How**: Diagnose deployment issue

```bash
# Check Kubernetes deployment
kubectl get pods -n aicart | grep gaming
kubectl logs -n aicart deployment/intelliverse-gaming-backend

# Check Ingress
kubectl get ingress -n aicart -o yaml | grep gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai

# Common fixes:
# - Scale deployment if scaled to 0
# - Fix image pull errors
# - Verify Ingress routing
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -I https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai
# Expected: HTTP/2 200
```

### 2. Add SEO metadata for gaming platform

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 2-3 hours
- **What**: Add title, meta description, OG tags, VideoGame schema
- **Why**: Enables indexing and social sharing
- **Where**: `intelliverse-gaming-backend/public/index.html` or frontend template
- **How**:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Gaming | IntelliVerse X - AI-Powered Educational Games</title>
    <meta name="description" content="Play AI-powered educational games on IntelliVerse X. Interactive learning through gaming, quizzes, and challenges.">
    
    <link rel="canonical" href="https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
    
    <meta property="og:title" content="Gaming | IntelliVerse X">
    <meta property="og:description" content="AI-powered educational games and interactive learning">
    <meta property="og:type" content="website">
    <meta property="og:url" content="https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
    
    <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
    <meta name="twitter:title" content="Gaming | IntelliVerse X">
    
    <script type="application/ld+json">
    {
      "@context": "https://schema.org",
      "@type": "VideoGame",
      "name": "IntelliVerse X Gaming Platform",
      "description": "AI-powered educational games for interactive learning",
      "applicationCategory": "GameApplication",
      "gamePlatform": ["Web Browser", "iOS", "Android"],
      "genre": ["Educational", "Puzzle", "Quiz"],
      "offers": {
        "@type": "Offer",
        "price": "0",
        "priceCurrency": "USD"
      }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
    <main>
        <h1>IntelliVerse X Gaming - Learn Through Play</h1>
        <p>Discover AI-powered educational games that make learning fun and engaging.</p>
        
        <section>
            <h2>Featured Games</h2>
            <ul>
                <li>Interactive Quizzes</li>
                <li>AI-Driven Challenges</li>
                <li>Educational Puzzles</li>
                <li>Progress Tracking</li>
            </ul>
        </section>
    </main>
</body>
</html>
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -E '<title>|VideoGame'
```

### 3. Add robots.txt and sitemap.xml

- **Priority**: P1 — estimated 30 minutes
- **What**: Create crawler control files
- **Where**: `intelliverse-gaming-backend/public/`
- **How**:

```
User-agent: *
Allow: /

User-agent: GPTBot
Allow: /

Sitemap: https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml
```

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
  <url>
    <loc>https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai/</loc>
    <lastmod>2026-05-17</lastmod>
    <changefreq>weekly</changefreq>
    <priority>1.0</priority>
  </url>
</urlset>
```

### 4. Add security headers

- **Priority**: P1 — estimated 30 minutes
- **What**: Configure HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, CSP
- **Where**: Nginx config or middleware
- **How**:

```nginx
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains" always;
add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
add_header X-Frame-Options "DENY" always;
add_header Referrer-Policy "no-referrer-when-downgrade" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';" always;
```

### 5. Add individual game schemas

- **Priority**: P1 — estimated 2-4 hours
- **What**: Add VideoGame schema to each game page
- **Where**: Game detail pages
- **How**:

```html
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "VideoGame",
  "name": "Math Quest Challenge",
  "description": "Solve math puzzles powered by AI in this educational adventure",
  "genre": "Educational",
  "gamePlatform": "Web Browser",
  "playMode": "SinglePlayer",
  "offers": {
    "@type": "Offer",
    "price": "0",
    "priceCurrency": "USD",
    "availability": "https://schema.org/InStock"
  }
}
</script>
```

### 6. Add llms.txt

- **Priority**: P2 — estimated 1 hour
- **What**: Create `/llms.txt` for GEO
- **Where**: `public/llms.txt`
- **How**:

```markdown
# IntelliVerse X Gaming

> AI-powered educational gaming platform featuring interactive quizzes, challenges, and learning games.

Key features:

- **Educational games**: Math, science, language learning through gameplay
- **AI adaptation**: Difficulty adjusts based on player performance
- **Progress tracking**: Monitor learning milestones
- **Multiplayer support**: Compete and collaborate with other learners

## Games

- [Main platform](https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai): Browse all available games
- [Quiz Platform](https://quiz.intelli-verse-x.ai): Interactive quiz games
- [Quests](https://quests.intelli-verse-x.ai): Educational quest challenges

## Related

- [IntelliVerse X Home](https://intelli-verse-x.ai): Ecosystem overview
```

### Scenario B: Coming soon page

If gaming platform is under construction:

### 1. Deploy holding page

- **Priority**: P0 — estimated 1 hour
- **What**: Serve "Coming Soon" page with noindex
- **How**:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Gaming | Coming Soon - IntelliVerse X</title>
    <meta name="description" content="IntelliVerse X gaming platform - launching soon">
    <meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow">
    
    <style>
        body {
            font-family: system-ui, sans-serif;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: center;
            min-height: 100vh;
            margin: 0;
            background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f093fb 0%, #f5576c 100%);
            color: white;
            text-align: center;
        }
        main { padding: 2rem; }
        h1 { font-size: 2.5rem; }
        a {
            display: inline-block;
            margin-top: 2rem;
            padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem;
            background: white;
            color: #f5576c;
            text-decoration: none;
            border-radius: 4px;
            font-weight: 600;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <main>
        <h1>🎮 Gaming Platform - Coming Soon</h1>
        <p>Get ready for AI-powered educational games!</p>
        <a href="https://intelli-verse-x.ai">Visit IntelliVerse X</a>
    </main>
</body>
</html>
```

---

### Scenario C: Redirect or remove

If gaming platform is not in roadmap:

### 1. Redirect to main domain

- **Priority**: P0 — estimated 30 minutes
- **What**: Set up 301 redirect
- **How** (Nginx):

```nginx
server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai;
    return 301 https://intelli-verse-x.ai$request_uri;
}
```

Or remove DNS record entirely.

## 5. Stack-aware notes

Backend service name suggests this might be a **backend API** (not frontend). If so:

1. **Separate frontend needed** — `intelliverse-gaming-backend` may only serve API endpoints (e.g., `/api/games`). A separate frontend (React/Vue/Next) is needed to serve the HTML landing page.

2. **Check if frontend repo exists** — Look for `intelliverse-gaming-frontend` or similar. The backend alone cannot provide SEO-friendly HTML.

3. **API-only architecture** — If this is purely an API service, consider:
   - Serve static marketing page via nginx at `/`
   - Proxy API routes to backend at `/api/*`
   - Or deploy separate frontend app

4. **Hybrid approach**:
   ```nginx
   location = / {
       root /var/www/gaming-public;
       try_files /index.html =404;
   }
   
   location /api {
       proxy_pass http://gaming-backend:8080;
   }
   ```

## 6. Verification plan

Same as products.md—see `remediation/products.md` section 6.

Key checks:
```bash
curl -I https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai  # expect 200, not 404
curl -sS https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep VideoGame
firecrawl scrape https://gaming.intelli-verse-x.ai --only-main-content
```

## 7. Open questions / blockers

1. **Which scenario applies?** — Active platform (A), under construction (B), or not needed (C)?

2. **Is there a frontend repo?** — Backend name suggests API-only. Need frontend for SEO.

3. **What games exist?** — If deploying Scenario A, what content should the landing page reference?

4. **Integration with quiz/quests subdomains?** — Are `gaming`, `quiz`, and `quests` separate apps or related? Consider cross-linking in llms.txt.

5. **Target audience** — Educational (K-12, higher ed) or general gaming? Affects metadata and schema choices.

## 8. Estimated impact

### Scenario A (active gaming platform)

**SEO**:
- **Before**: 0/10 — HTTP 404
- **After**: 7-8/10 — indexed with VideoGame schemas
- **Timeline**: 3-7 days indexing, 2-4 weeks ranking impact

**GEO**:
- **Before**: 0/10 — no information
- **After**: 7/10 — LLMs can describe gaming offerings
- **Timeline**: 4-8 weeks (LLM crawler cycles)

**Traffic**:
- **Before**: 0 visitors/month
- **After**: 50-200 organic visitors/month (assuming 5+ games, educational keywords)

### Scenario B (coming soon)

**SEO**: 0/10 → 3/10 (HTTP 200 but noindex, no search benefit)
**GEO**: 0/10 → 2/10 (LLMs know it exists but not yet functional)
**Traffic**: ~5-10 visitors/month (direct only)

### Scenario C (redirect)

**SEO**: 0/10 → N/A (no standalone ranking, consolidates to main site)
**Traffic**: Redirected to main domain
